title: The lock flag is implemented with inverted semantics relative to its protection comment
reason: Merged into F-003 because the misleading `toggleLock()` semantics materially increase exposure to the timer-reset lockup, but the comment inversion alone is not the main protocol harm.

title: Authorized third parties can force a matured warmup claim into the wrong asset form
reason: Does not create direct fund loss, insolvency, or lockup; it is primarily a preference and integration-friction issue.

title: secondsToNextEpoch reverts exactly when the protocol most needs it
reason: Informational helper behavior only; not a reportable security issue.

title: Forfeit function loses rebased tokens during warmup
reason: Matches the contract's explicit early-exit semantics: forfeiting returns principal while abandoning warmup rewards. That is a design choice, not an unintended vulnerability.

title: setWarmupLength retroactively modifies all existing warmup periods
reason: Incorrect. Existing claims use the stored per-position `expiry`, and `claim()` does not reference the current `warmupPeriod`.

title: Unstake lacks slippage protection against rebase volatility
reason: Incorrect framing. `unstake()` redeems a deterministic token amount, not a slippage-prone trade, and no negative-rebase mechanism is shown in scope.

title: Insufficient FLOOR balance can cause permanent unstake DoS
reason: This is a consequence of insolvency or out-of-scope privileged abuse, not an independent flaw in `unstake()`. The in-scope accounting root cause is captured in F-001.

title: Missing zero-amount validation in stake function
reason: Merged into F-003 because the missing check matters insofar as it makes the warmup timer-reset attack nearly free.

title: toggleLock can be used to grief own warmup position
reason: Incorrect characterization. The meaningful issue is third-party access plus expiry reset, which is captured in F-003.

title: wrap and unwrap lack allowance validation
reason: Standard ERC20 allowance failures already revert; this is a UX concern, not a security finding.
